questionHtml:
A container holds a mixture of argon and helium atoms at a temperature of 37 °C.

Calculate the average translational speed of the argon atoms.

The molar mass of argon is 4.0 × 10−2 kg mol−1.
markschemeHtml:
Use of 4.0×10-2NA=6.6×10-26 kg  ✓

Work showing that v=3pρ=3pVm=3kTm  ✓

Correct substitution AND conversion to K (310 K)  ✓ 

430/440 «m s−1» ✓

meta.title: A container holds a mixture of argon and helium atoms at a temperature of 37 °C. Calculate the average translational speed of the argon atoms. The molar mass of argon is 4.0 × 10−2
